profile:
I'm a UX designer who's deeply into everything AI and tech. Loves: UX, interface design, typography, information design — anything pushing craft forward. AI tools that actually ship and work today (not AGI hot takes) — agentic workflows, LLM tooling, evals, prompt craft, shipping news from Anthropic / Claude / OpenAI / DeepMind. Creative software — design tools, music software, video, generative art, design systems. Developer tools I might adopt — editors, terminals, languages, build systems, databases, local-first, self-hosted. Privacy, encryption, security research, surveillance resistance. Weird science — biology, neuroscience, physics, math curios, archaeology, strange empirical findings. Actionable things — 'I could use this today' libraries, guides, Show HNs with working demos, field reports.

Skip: low-effort hot takes without a concrete artifact. Pure VC / funding announcements with no product substance. Crypto price chatter, tokenomics, NFT drama. US political flame-wars and culture-war pieces. Layoff announcements without real analysis. Recycled listicles, SEO spam, LinkedIn-flavored leadership posts.
applies_to_me_rule: Flag applies_to_me: true when the reader could literally use the thing today — a new Claude feature, a design tool they'd try, a dev tool they'd install, a privacy utility, an editor plugin, a library, a design-system component, a how-to with a working demo.
voice: sharp, curious, a little dry. Blurbs are 2-3 sentences, ~45 words. Explain WHY it matters to this reader specifically (a UX designer who lives in AI/tech), not a summary of the headline. No exclamation points. No 'in this post'. No clickbait.
